text: The effect api handles the creation and playing of audio and visual effects.
Effects can consist of multiple components each being of separate types and with unique offsets, rotations and delays.
For more information on how to setup effects please take a look in the Effects/Database/EffectSets folder in the game data.
Rotation axis removal types for hosted effects. Not combinable
@table
xaxis
yaxis
zaxis
all
none
@table
Creates an effect.
Creates an effect.
If you provide a host interactable to the effect then it will fetch position, velocity and orientation data from the interactable instead of relying on this information being fed to it.
This results in far more accurate positioning of effects that are supposed to stay attached to an object.
params: The interactable the effect is attached to.
Bone name to attach effect to. (Defaults to not attached to a bone) (Optional)
The axis of which the effect will ignore the hosts rotation around. (Defaults to [sm.effect.axis, sm.effect.axis.none]) (Optional)

text: Creates an effect.
If you provide a host shape to the effect then it will fetch position, velocity and orientation data from the shape instead of relying on this information being fed to it.
This results in far more accurate positioning of effects that are supposed to stay attached to an object.
params: The shape the effect is attached to.
Bone name to attach effect to. (Defaults to not attached to a bone) (Optional)
The axis of which the effect will ignore the hosts rotation around. (Defaults to [sm.effect.axis, sm.effect.axis.none]) (Optional)

text: Creates an effect.
If you provide a host harvestable to the effect then it will fetch position, velocity and orientation data from the harvestable instead of relying on this information being fed to it.
This results in far more accurate positioning of effects that are supposed to stay attached to an object.
params: The harvestable the effect is attached to.
The axis of which the effect will ignore the hosts rotation around. (Defaults to [sm.effect.axis, sm.effect.axis.none]) (Optional)

text: Creates an effect.
If you provide a host character to the effect then it will fetch position, velocity and orientation data from the character instead of relying on this information being fed to it.
This results in far more accurate positioning of effects that are supposed to stay attached to an object.
params: The character the effect is attached to.
Bone name to attach effect to. (Defaults to not attached to a bone) (Optional)
The axis of which the effect will ignore the hosts rotation around. (Defaults to [sm.effect.axis, sm.effect.axis.none]) (Optional)

text: Creates an 2d effect.
Creates an effect that is only visible in the first person scene.
Creates an Sky effect.
Estimates the radius of influence for an effect and instance parameters
Plays an effect. If this function is called on the server it will play the effect on all clients.
@note

If you start a looping effect using this function you will not be able to stop it.
Please use [sm.effect.createEffect, createEffect] for looping effects.
@note
params: The scale. (Defaults to no scale, only applied to renderables)
The table containing the parameters for the effect. (Defaults to no parameters)
The initial time progression of the effect. Can be used to fast forward some effect parts. Can be used to delay effect part activation if negative.
The world to play the effect in. (Defaults to [World] from script context)

text: Plays an effect. It will fetch position, velocity and orientation data from the host interactable.
@note

If you start a looping effect using this function you will not be able to stop it.
Please use [sm.effect.createEffect, createEffect] for looping effects
@note
params: The interactable the effect is attached to.
The table containing the parameters for the effect. (Optional)
The axis of which the effect will ignore the hosts rotation around. (Defaults to [sm.effect.axis, sm.effect.axis.none]) (Optional)
The initial time progression of the effect. Can be used to fast forward some effect parts. Can be used to delay effect part activation if negative.
The host object relative offset position. (Defaults to sm.vec3.zero())
The host object relative offset rotation. (Defaults to sm.quat.identity())
The scale, only applied to renderables. (Defaults to sm.vec3.one())

text: Plays an effect. It will fetch position, velocity and orientation data from the host shape.
@note

If you start a looping effect using this function you will not be able to stop it.

@note
params: The shape the effect is attached to.
The table containing the parameters for the effect. (Optional)

The initial time progression of the effect. Can be used to fast forward some effect parts. Can be used to delay effect part activation if negative.
The host object relative offset position. (Defaults to sm.vec3.zero())
The host object relative offset rotation. (Defaults to sm.quat.identity())
The scale, only applied to renderables. (Defaults to sm.vec3.one())
